plotHooks.title: Black Envelope Mystery
plotHooks.description: The Executor passes around a sealed black envelope containing J. Baroo’s codicil. During the blackout the envelope disappears. A fake envelope is later discovered in the garden. The real envelope is hidden in a statue.
plotHooks.resolution: The Director stole the real envelope during the blackout and hid it inside The Blind Judge statue. He used a fake envelope to mislead the guests.

plotHooks.title: Cow Bell Code
plotHooks.description: Baroo Manor operates with an old cow bell service code. The host rings once for drinks before dinner. Players must deduce that two rings will unlock the dining wing.
plotHooks.resolution: The clapper hidden in the kitchen must be attached to the bell. Ringing twice summons The Maid to unlock the wing.

plotHooks.title: Director’s Hidden Motive
plotHooks.description: The Director appears calm and supportive but privately despises the Son and wants control of Baroo Holdings. He drafted a fake codicil that creates deadlock and elevates him to board controller.
plotHooks.resolution: Emails and the fake codicil reveal the Director’s scheme. The real codicil bars him from control, which is why he killed the Executor and hid the document.

plotHooks.title: Illegitimate Heir
plotHooks.description: A possible child of J. Baroo claims recognition and threatens the legitimacy of the family. Letters and evidence suggest there may be truth to the claim.
plotHooks.resolution: The real codicil acknowledges the Bastard Child with a settlement but preserves the principal inheritance for the legitimate heirs.
